state_description: Coarse cropping/horticulture activity bundle with farm fuel use, irrigation/pumping electricity and process emissions dominated by soils and fertiliser-related nitrous oxide.
Cropping/horticulture activity bundle with improved fertiliser management, moderate electrification and lower process emissions intensity.
Coarse livestock activity bundle dominated by methane and nitrous oxide emissions, plus diesel/electricity/gas use on farms.
Livestock activity bundle with representative methane, manure and productivity improvements plus moderate on-farm energy efficiency.
Representative commercial building-service bundle with current electricity, gas and liquid-fuel shares from official energy data.
Commercial service bundle with higher-efficiency equipment, reduced fossil fuel use and a predominantly electric load.
Highly electrified commercial building-service bundle with very low direct fossil use and lower final-energy intensity.
Representative residential building-service bundle with current electricity, gas, liquid-fuel and biomass shares from official energy data.
Residential services with strong heat-pump and appliance-efficiency uptake but some residual gas or biomass in the near term.
Highly electrified residential service bundle with widespread heat pumps and high shell/appliance efficiency.

state_label: Incumbent cement with current clinker ratio
state_description: Reduced-form incumbent Australian cement-equivalent supply with current clinker ratio and recalibrated mixed-fuel kiln use for better national commodity closure.
balance_tuning_note: Incumbent kiln fuel mix re-tuned in the balance-ready pack to improve 2025 commodity closure while preserving the conventional cement route.

state_description: Cement-equivalent production with lower clinker factor, higher SCM use and substantially higher alternative-fuel/biomass substitution.
Low-clinker cement route with high alternative-fuel use and substantial CO2 capture and storage from clinker production.
National average electricity supply dominated by the incumbent thermal fleet with declining coal share and some gas backup.
balance_tuning_note: 2025 electricity intensity nudged to the official benchmark average so that default incumbent generation activity reproduces the national 2025 electricity emissions total.
benchmark_balance_note: Electricity-sector coal and gas inputs are generation fuels, not end-use final energy. Use electricity_balance_2025.csv for generation-output closure and commodity_balance_2025.csv for final electricity closure.

state_description: National reduced-form frontier broadly consistent with AEMO/CSIRO planning: VRE, storage, transmission and gas backup under implemented-policy decarbonisation.
benchmark_balance_note: Electricity-sector coal and gas inputs are generation fuels, not end-use final energy. Use electricity_balance_2025.csv for generation-output closure and commodity_balance_2025.csv for final electricity closure.

state_description: More conservative high-firming near-zero power system state with extra storage/firming and very low direct emissions intensity.
benchmark_balance_note: Electricity-sector coal and gas inputs are generation fuels, not end-use final energy. Use electricity_balance_2025.csv for generation-output closure and commodity_balance_2025.csv for final electricity closure.

state_description: Reduced-form incumbent high-temperature industrial heat with a recalibrated national-average mix of coal, natural gas and existing biomass/other industrial bioenergy use.
balance_tuning_note: Fuel mix re-tuned in the balance-ready pack to reduce 2025 coal overstatement and improve national commodity closure. Treat as a calibration-oriented incumbent rather than a literal subsector average.

state_description: High-temperature industrial heat from electric furnaces, plasma/resistive routes or other deep-electrification options.
High-temperature heat from hydrogen and/or biomass where direct electrification is not preferred.
Representative low-temperature industrial heat from gas-fired boilers and process heaters.
Low-temperature industrial heat from heat pumps and resistive/electric process heating.
Low-temperature industrial heat supplied by renewable hydrogen and/or sustainable biomass where electrification is constrained.
Reduced-form incumbent medium-temperature industrial heat with a recalibrated national-average mix of natural gas, limited coal and existing biomass/other industrial bioenergy use.
balance_tuning_note: Fuel mix re-tuned in the balance-ready pack to reduce 2025 coal overstatement and improve national commodity closure. Treat as a calibration-oriented incumbent rather than a literal subsector average.

state_description: Medium-temperature industrial heat supplied by electric boilers, advanced heat pumps and resistive process heating.
Medium-temperature heat supplied by hydrogen and biomass where direct electrification is less attractive.
Exploratory engineered removal state representing direct air capture with permanent storage under large cost uncertainty.
Reduced-form biological removal state representing land-sector sequestration / carbon-forestry style removals with strong land-use and permanence caveats.
Conventional diesel road freight service benchmark, spanning medium and heavy road freight at a national-average level.
Improved diesel road freight with better vehicle efficiency, logistics and utilisation but still liquid-fuel based.
Battery-electric road freight service suitable first for urban, depot-based and shorter-haul tasks and later for a wider share of freight.
Hydrogen fuel-cell freight service for longer-range or harder-to-electrify road freight tasks.
Stock-average passenger road fleet dominated by internal-combustion vehicles. Calibrated to aggregate road-energy use rather than only new-vehicle test values.
Passenger road fleet with widespread hybridisation and improved efficiency but continued reliance on liquid fuels.
Predominantly battery-electric passenger road service with lower final energy demand and zero direct tailpipe emissions.
Conventional blast-furnace / basic-oxygen-furnace crude steel route using iron ore, coal and some gas, with small scrap input.
benchmark_balance_note: For 2025 benchmark commodity closure, BF-BOF coal is mapped 40% to final-energy coal and 60% to process_reductant_coal. Gross model input demand still retains 100% of the coal input for pricing and process representation.

state_description: Crude steel from high-scrap electric arc furnace route with modest auxiliary gas use.
Primary steel route retaining BF/BOF logic but with substantial capture and storage, lower residual emissions and higher power use.
benchmark_balance_note: For 2025 benchmark commodity closure, BF-BOF coal is mapped 40% to final-energy coal and 60% to process_reductant_coal. Gross model input demand still retains 100% of the coal input for pricing and process representation.

state_description: Near-zero primary steel archetype covering hydrogen direct reduced iron with electric melting/finishing (EAF or electric smelting family).
